script:
export const meta = {
  name: 's2s3-postmortem',
  description: 'Root-cause WHY S2/S3 silently lost source metadata (DOI/journal/tier) it plainly had, and design a GENERAL fail-loud invariant + regression test so this class of silent data-loss can never recur on any query.',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Root cause', detail: 'read the real S2/S3 code: where metadata SHOULD be captured and why it was not' },
    { title: 'Prevention', detail: 'design a query-agnostic fail-loud invariant + regression test' },
    { title: 'Gate', detail: 'Fable: is the cause real + is the guard genuinely general, not overfit', model: 'fable' },
  ],
}

const CTX = `
POST-MORTEM. What went wrong (measured, on the real 329-basket corpus): S2/S3 produced a cp3 corpus
where only 3/995 evidence rows had a DOI and 3/995 had a journal name, and 253/995 were tier=UNKNOWN
— even though the metadata was PLAINLY PRESENT in each row's own direct_quote text and source_url
(e.g. Acemoglu-Restrepo's row literally contains "Journal of Economic Perspectives" and DOI
10.1257/jep.33.2.3, yet its doi/journal fields were blank). A trivial downstream deterministic pass
(regex DOI + domain->venue map) recovered doi 3->240, journal 3->294, and cut UNKNOWN 253->145.
So S2/S3 SILENTLY DROPPED metadata it had in hand. Consequences: good peer-reviewed sources got
mislabeled UNKNOWN (looks low-quality), instruction-following on journal-demanding tasks tanked, and
nothing anywhere FAILED or warned — the corpus looked fine.

READ-ONLY: read the S2/S3 code but DO NOT WRITE to /home/polaris/wt/s2s3 (a fix wheel is editing it).
Read from /workspace/POLARIS/src (or the s2s3 worktree) read-only; write any findings to
/home/polaris/polaris_project/s2s3_postmortem.md ONLY. The proven recovery logic is at
/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/scripts/repair_corpus_metadata.py. Corpus: cp3_basket_snapshot.json
(995 rows). Be concrete: file:line for the failure. Honest — if the extractor was never written vs
written-but-never-called vs silently-excepted, say which.
`

const CAUSE_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object', required: ['mechanism', 'file_line', 'failure_class'],
  properties: {
    mechanism: { type: 'string', description: 'the ACTUAL reason metadata was lost — at file:line' },
    file_line: { type: 'string' },
    failure_class: { enum: ['never_written', 'written_never_called', 'silently_excepted', 'dropped_in_transform', 'wrong_default', 'other'] },
    was_silent: { type: 'boolean', description: 'did anything fail/warn, or did the bad corpus look fine' },
    other_silent_losses: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'other places in S2/S3 that could silently drop data' },
    evidence: { type: 'string' },
  },
}
const PREVENT_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object', required: ['invariant', 'is_query_agnostic', 'test'],
  properties: {
    invariant: { type: 'string', description: 'the fail-loud check: e.g. post-S3, if direct_quote matches DOI_RE then doi MUST be set; if domain in KNOWN_JOURNALS then tier != UNKNOWN — fail loud with counts' },
    is_query_agnostic: { type: 'boolean', description: 'does it work for ANY topic/query, not just AI-labor' },
    test: { type: 'string', description: 'the regression test that would have caught this, and catches it on any future query' },
    where_it_runs: { type: 'string', description: 'where in the pipeline the invariant is enforced so it triggers on every run' },
    false_positive_risk: { type: 'string' },
  },
}

phase('Root cause')
const cause = await agent(
  `${CTX}\n\nROOT-CAUSE it. Find where S2 stamps/enriches evidence rows and where S3 consolidates them.
Determine EXACTLY why doi/journal stayed blank and tier stayed UNKNOWN when the data was in the row's
own text. Was the extractor never written? written but never called? called but silently swallowed an
exception? populated then dropped in a later transform/serialization? a wrong default? Give the
mechanism at file:line, whether it failed SILENTLY, and list any OTHER spots in S2/S3 that could
silently drop data the same way.`,
  { label: 's2s3:root-cause', phase: 'Root cause', schema: CAUSE_SCHEMA },
)

phase('Prevention')
const prevent = await agent(
  `${CTX}\n\nGiven the root cause:\n${JSON.stringify(cause, null, 2)}
Design the PREVENTION so this class of silent metadata-loss can NEVER recur on ANY query. It must be:
(1) a FAIL-LOUD INVARIANT enforced on every S2/S3 run — e.g. after consolidation, assert that any row
whose direct_quote/url contains a DOI pattern has doi populated, and any row from a known-journal
domain is not tier=UNKNOWN; if violated, FAIL LOUD with the offending counts (not a silent pass).
(2) QUERY-AGNOSTIC — it must hold for a finance query, a medical query, anything, not just AI-labor.
Use structural signals (DOI regex, domain class), never topic keywords.
(3) a REGRESSION TEST that reproduces this exact failure and would have caught it, and keeps catching
it. State WHERE the invariant runs so it fires on every future corpus build. Note false-positive risk
(e.g. a genuine non-journal source correctly having no DOI must not trip it).`,
  { label: 's2s3:prevention', phase: 'Prevention', schema: PREVENT_SCHEMA },
)

phase('Gate')
const gate = await agent(
  `${CTX}\n\nYOU ARE FABLE. Judge the post-mortem.
ROOT CAUSE: ${JSON.stringify(cause, null, 2)}
PREVENTION: ${JSON.stringify(prevent, null, 2)}
Answer: (1) Is the root cause the REAL mechanism (file:line verified), or a plausible guess? (2) Is the
prevention GENUINELY query-agnostic and fail-loud — would it catch this on a finance/medical corpus
too, and would it have failed the original bad run instead of passing it silently? (3) Is there a
BROADER lesson — is silent data-loss-without-failure a pattern elsewhere in the pipeline (the corpus
'looked fine' while being broken)? (4) The single most important guard to add so 'looks fine but is
silently broken' cannot ship again. Be blunt; the operator called this a fatal, stupid mistake and
wants it structurally impossible to repeat.`,
  { label: 's2s3:fable-gate', phase: 'Gate', model: 'fable', effort: 'high' },
)

log(`S2S3 postmortem: class=${cause?.failure_class} silent=${cause?.was_silent} | guard query-agnostic=${prevent?.is_query_agnostic}`)
return { cause, prevent, gate }

cause.mechanism: The DOI/journal/tier extractor for the S2/S3 path was NEVER WRITTEN. S2 (scripts/s2_select_replay.py HEAD) builds the cp2 kept-row pool with zero metadata enrichment: pass-through rows are appended raw (line 252 `kept_rows.append(row)`) and screened rows go through `ls.apply_result_to_row(row, res)` (line 268), which is defined in src/polaris_graph/retrieval/line_screen.py:1361-1386 and only rewrites the body field + attaches a line_screen sidecar — it does `dict(row)` (preserving any doi/journal already present) but never parses direct_quote/source_url to derive doi/journal/tier. There is no import of any extractor at HEAD. S3 (scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py HEAD) then only reads the fields S2 never filled: `_tier(i)` at line 101-102 returns `str(rows[i].get('tier','') or 'UNKNOWN')` — a wrong-default that silently coerces every blank tier to 'UNKNOWN' (that single `or 'UNKNOWN'` is the 253 UNKNOWN) — and there is no `_doi`/`_journal` accessor and no member_dois/member_journals at HEAD at all, so DOI/journal were never even surfaced. The DOI in source_url (e.g. .../doi/10.1257/jep.33.2.3) and the 'Journal of Economic Perspectives' string in direct_quote were in hand on every row and never read. The ~5 rows that arrived pre-populated upstream (acemoglu) flowed through intact, proving it is absence-of-extraction, not a transform drop.
cause.file_line: scripts/s2_select_replay.py:248-268 (no enrichment; apply_result_to_row at src/polaris_graph/retrieval/line_screen.py:1361-1386) and scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py:101-102 (_tier ... or 'UNKNOWN'; no _doi/_journal)
cause.evidence: Confirmed against committed HEAD of /home/polaris/wt/s2s3 (working tree is mid-fix by the wheel: it adds untracked src/polaris_graph/retrieval/source_metadata.py + enrich_row_metadata calls — the fix, not the bug). HEAD s2_select_replay.py has no metadata extractor import and no doi/journal/tier writes; apply_result_to_row (line_screen.py:1361-1386) touches only body+sidecar. HEAD s3_consolidate_replay.py grep shows only `def _tier` (101) and `member_tiers_weight` (177) — no _doi/_journal/member_dois. The shipped outputs prove silence: outputs/s2s3_repass/s2/cp2_corpus_snapshot.json has no metadata_enrichment_summary; outputs/s2s3_repass/s3/cp3_basket_snapshot.json has no corpus_metadata_summary and baskets lack member_dois. Measured on that cp2 (728 rows): only 5 doi, only 5 journal, 177 UNKNOWN; 723 blank-doi rows, 156 with a DOI directly regex-recoverable from their own source_url/direct_quote (21 from url, 135 from quote); blank rows (e.g. ev_454) don't even carry a doi/journal key. Nothing raised or warned; stage summaries never measured population. Full write-up: /home/polaris/polaris_project/s2s3_postmortem.md
other_silent_losses: scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py:102 _tier(...) or 'UNKNOWN' — wrong-default silently laundering any missing tier into UNKNOWN
scripts/s2_select_replay.py ~line 221 topic-judge except Exception (fail-open) — any judge error silently yields zero off-subject stamps
scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py ~line 142 _stmt bare except around extract_numeric_claims — swallows extraction errors, silent fallback to weaker representative
scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py ~line 63 _nli_score_stats except Exception — semantic-merge telemetry can silently become unavailable
scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py ~line 351 unified_deletion_disclosure except Exception — swallows S2 summary.json read/parse errors

Row schema by omission: blank rows (e.g. ev_454) carry no doi/journal key at all; every .get('doi','') consumer sees empty and no S2/S3 code backfills, so the gap is invisible without a population lock-bar that HEAD never had

prevent.invariant: Add assert_metadata_invariants(rows, stage) to src/polaris_graph/retrieval/source_metadata.py: a per-row STRUCTURAL RESIDUAL check (not a population threshold). R1: if DOI_RE.search(direct_quote OR source_url) matches, doi MUST be non-empty. R2: if domain_of(url) is in DOMAIN_TIER (known scholarly/gov/venue set), tier MUST NOT be UNKNOWN/blank. On any violation it raises SystemExit with the offending counts and example evidence_ids (e.g. 'rows with a DOI in their own quote/url but doi BLANK: 156', 'rows on a known-journal domain but tier=UNKNOWN: 47'). Because the guard recomputes with the SAME DOI_RE/DOMAIN_TIER the extractor uses, extractor and check cannot disagree — a non-zero residual is a proof the extractor did not run on those rows, i.e. the exact HEAD bug. Measured: fires on every bug-state cp2 (140-240 DOI + 44-52 tier residuals -> hard fail); reads 0/0 on the enriched cp2 (s2s3_meta). The existing wheel fix only MEASURES population (s2_select_replay.py:275-299, s3_consolidate_replay.py:223-251) with no assert/raise — this converts measurement into a fail-loud gate. A threshold on doi_populated cannot be used because population is query-dependent; only the per-row residual is safe.
prevent.where_it_runs: Both stage write-sites so it fires on every corpus build. (1) S2 scripts/s2_select_replay.py right after the lock-bar block (after line 293, before cp2['evidence_for_gen']=kept_rows at ~297): cp2['metadata_invariant']=assert_metadata_invariants(kept_rows, stage='s2') — gates cp2 at the disk write, and kept_rows includes the pass-through branch that was the original blank-row source. (2) S3 scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py right after corpus_metadata_summary is built (after line 251): assert_metadata_invariants(rows, stage='s3') over the full cp2 pool S3 consumes — catches a cp2 produced by an old/rogue pre-guard S2 and refuses to consolidate rather than laundering blanks into UNKNOWN at _tier(...) or 'UNKNOWN' (s3:112-113).
prevent.false_positive_risk: Bounded and measured to zero on this corpus. R1 fires ONLY when a DOI is literally present in the row's own text/url, so genuine non-journal sources with no DOI (blogs, press releases, gov landing pages) never trip — the 480-754 legitimately DOI-less rows per corpus all pass (0 false positives confirmed across 7 corpora). R2 is a whitelist: a finance/medical domain not yet in DOMAIN_TIER is simply not checked by R2 (still fully protected by the universal R1), so unknown domains cannot false-fire. Residual R2 edge case: a known-journal domain whose specific URL is a non-article page (publisher About page) would be tiered by publisher tier — acceptable and deterministic, so check and extractor still agree. Extractor and check share DOI_RE/DOMAIN_TIER, eliminating any independent-regex drift class of false positive.
prevent.test: tests/test_s2s3_metadata_invariant.py with three cases. test_head_bug_state_fails_loud_without_enrichment builds the EXACT HEAD failure (Acemoglu row: DOI in direct_quote, JEP on aeaweb.org, but doi/journal blank and tier absent; plus a medical NEJM DOI row on pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov in the same blank state; plus a legit DOI-less blog) and asserts assert_metadata_invariants raises SystemExit naming 'doi BLANK: 2' and 'tier=UNKNOWN: 2' (blog excluded) — this is red at HEAD, catching the bug. test_enrichment_then_invariant_passes runs enrich_row_metadata then the invariant, asserts 0/0 residuals and that rows[0].doi=='10.1257/jep.33.2.3', journal=='Journal of Economic Perspectives', and the medical rows[1].doi=='10.1056/NEJMoa2035389' (proving query-agnostic recovery) while the blog stays doi==''. test_blog_never_false_positives locks the false-positive boundary. The check being structural (DOI regex + domain class, no topic keywords) means it keeps catching this class on any future query, finance or medical or otherwise.

result.gate:
VERDICT: The post-mortem is accurate. I independently re-verified every load-bearing claim against committed HEAD (7b72d41) and the shipped artifacts. Detail per question:

(1) ROOT CAUSE — REAL, verified, not a guess.
- `git show HEAD:scripts/s2_select_replay.py`: pass-through rows appended raw at line 252 (`kept_rows.append(row)`), screened rows via `ls.apply_result_to_row(row, res)` at line 268. Zero grep hits for doi/journal/enrich/source_metadata anywhere in HEAD S2. Confirmed.
- `apply_result_to_row` is at HEAD `src/polaris_graph/retrieval/line_screen.py:1361-1386` exactly as claimed: `dict(row)` copy, rewrites the widest body field + attaches `line_screen` sidecar, derives nothing. Confirmed.
- HEAD `scripts/s3_consolidate_replay.py:101-102`: `_tier` with `or 'UNKNOWN'`; HEAD has no `_doi`/`_journal`/`member_dois` (only `_tier`:101 and `member_tiers_weight`:177). The working tree now has `_doi` (~line 115) and `member_dois`/`member_journals` (198-199) — that is the wheel's in-progress fix, correctly distinguished from the bug. Confirmed.
- Failure class `never_written` is the honest call: no extractor existed at HEAD to be uncalled or to except. `src/polaris_graph/retrieval/source_metadata.py` is untracked (new), i.e. the fix.
- Measurements reproduced exactly: shipped `outputs/s2s3_repass/s2/cp2_corpus_snapshot.json` = 728 rows, 5 doi, 5 journal, 177 UNKNOWN, 156 regex-recoverable DOIs; no `metadata_enrichment_summary`. Shipped iter7 cp3 (329 baskets, 385 members, 95 UNKNOWN) has no `member_dois` and no `corpus_metadata_summary`. The repair script header independently corroborates 992/995 blank, 253 UNKNOWN on the outline-agent corpus. `was_silent: true` is correct — nothing in HEAD measures field population, only row counts.
- All six "other silent losses" check out at HEAD: s2:221 fail-open judge except, s3:63, s3:153 (claimed ~142), s3:362 (claimed ~351) — two line numbers drift by ~10 but are flagged as approximate.

(2) PREVENTION — genuinely query-agnostic and fail-loud AS DESIGNED, with one material caveat: IT IS NOT IMPLEMENTED YET. `assert_metadata_invariants` exists nowhere in the tree, and `tests/test_s2s3_metadata_invariant.py` does not exist — the prevention text reads as shipped but is a spec. That said, I reproduced its claimed measurements myself using the wheel's own `DOI_RE`/`DOMAIN_TIER`: on the bug-state cp2, R1 residual = 156, R2 residual = 47 (inside the claimed 140-240 / 44-52 ranges) — so the guard WOULD have hard-failed the original run instead of passing it. It is query-agnostic: R1 is pure structure (a DOI in the row's own bytes → doi must be populated), which fires identically on a finance (10.1111/jofi...) or medical (10.1056/NEJMoa...) corpus; R2 is whitelist-bounded so unknown domains cannot false-fire. The shared-DOI_RE design eliminates check/extractor drift — the residual is a proof the extractor didn't run. Two gaps worth naming: (a) sharing DOI_RE also shares blind spots (a too-narrow regex passes both sides silently — acceptable, since R1 still catches "extractor never ran", the actual bug class); (b) the invariant guards doi and tier but NOT journal population directly, and journal (3→294 recovered) was a large part of the loss — a known-journal-domain-but-journal-blank residual should be added as R3. The critique of the wheel fix is correct and important: I verified the current working-tree diff only computes and prints summaries — no assert, no raise. As it stands, a future enrichment regression would ship silently again.

(3) BROADER LESSON — yes, this is a pattern, verified in-tree. Four instances of the same shape at HEAD: fail-open `except Exception` on the topic judge (s2:221 — a judge outage yields zero off-subject stamps, indistinguishable from a clean corpus), swallowed `extract_numeric_claims` errors (s3:153), swallowed NLI telemetry (s3:63), swallowed S2-summary read errors (s3:362), plus two non-except variants: the wrong-default `or 'UNKNOWN'` that launders absence into a legitimate-looking category, and schema-by-omission (blank rows carry no doi/journal key at all, so every `.get('doi','')` consumer sees a valid-looking empty string). The unifying defect: stage summaries measure FLOW (n_sources_kept, n_dropped) but never CONTENT (field population), so a corpus can be structurally intact and semantically gutted while every gauge reads green.

(4) SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT GUARD — the per-row structural-residual invariant at every snapshot WRITE site, fail-closed: no stage may serialize a corpus containing a row whose own bytes prove a derivable field is blank. This is the right single guard because it is self-proving (recomputed from the row itself, no threshold, no query dependence) and it sits at the only chokepoint every corpus must pass (the disk write). Concretely: land `assert_metadata_invariants` exactly as specced — S2 after the lock-bar before the cp2 write, S3 over the consumed pool before consolidation — and add the R3 journal residual. The generalization the operator should demand: every "summary" block any stage writes must be an asserted contract, not telemetry; and the fail-open `except Exception` sites must each write a counted, disclosed residual that a downstream gate checks is zero or explained. Priority order: (1) commit the invariant + red-at-HEAD test (currently vaporware), (2) convert the wheel's print-only lock-bars into raises, (3) sweep the four except sites.

Files: /home/polaris/wt/s2s3 (HEAD 7b72d41, read-only), /home/polaris/wt/s2s3/outputs/s2s3_repass/s2/cp2_corpus_snapshot.json, /home/polaris/wt/s2s3/outputs/s2s3_repass/iter7/s3/cp3_basket_snapshot.json, /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/scripts/repair_corpus_metadata.py, /home/polaris/polaris_project/s2s3_postmortem.md.

logs: S2S3 postmortem: class=never_written silent=true | guard query-agnostic=true
summary: Root-cause WHY S2/S3 silently lost source metadata (DOI/journal/tier) it plainly had, and design a GENERAL fail-loud invariant + regression test so this class of silent data-loss can never recur on any query.

phases.title: Root cause
phases.detail: read the real S2/S3 code: where metadata SHOULD be captured and why it was not

phases.title: Prevention
phases.detail: design a query-agnostic fail-loud invariant + regression test

phases.title: Gate
phases.detail: Fable: is the cause real + is the guard genuinely general, not overfit
